data.title: As a Java developer, blast-radius and dependency-check gates recognize Java import and class-instantiation patterns
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend gate-2d-dependency-check.sh to extract Java import statements (import com.example.Foo pattern) and gate-2b-blast-radius.sh to recognize Java fan-in patterns (import statements and class instantiation). Both scripts treat java-maven and java-gradle as equivalent per the equivalence contract established in Story F1.

**Why**: gate-2d today skips Java files entirely when extracting dependencies. gate-2b does not recognize Java class instantiation or import patterns. Java PRs receive no dependency analysis or blast-radius scoring.

**Scope**:
- IN: gate-2d-dependency-check.sh: Java import statement extraction (import <package.Class> pattern); gate-2b-blast-radius.sh: Java fan-in patterns added as bash array elements (import pattern, new ClassName() pattern); both using bash array elements (NOT eval); both treating java-maven and java-gradle as equivalent (no if/else on stack value for these scripts)
- OUT: onboarding defaults (Story F2); detect-stack.sh (Story F1); project-detect.sh (Story F4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, gate-2d-dependency-check.sh extracts Java import statements from a Java source file fixture (e.g., 'import org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication' correctly identified as a dependency)
  ← Satisfies: "gate-2d-dependency-check.sh adds Java import statement extraction"
- When this story is complete, gate-2b-blast-radius.sh identifies fan-in from a Java file that uses 'import com.example.Foo' or 'new Foo()' patterns; the patterns are defined as bash array elements (no eval)
  ← Satisfies: "gate-2b-blast-radius.sh adds Java fan-in patterns (import, class instantiation) as bash array elements (not eval)"
- When this story is complete, both scripts apply the same Java logic regardless of whether detect-stack.sh returns java-maven or java-gradle (equivalence contract)
  ← Satisfies: "scripts treating both as equivalent via equivalence contract"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for Java import extraction in gate-2d and Java fan-in pattern recognition in gate-2b

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Story F3 depends on Story F1 (equivalence contract is defined there); do not branch on java-maven vs java-gradle in these gate scripts
- [Maintainability] Use bash array syntax for fan-in patterns (e.g., JAVA_PATTERNS=("import " "new "); for pattern in "${JAVA_PATTERNS[@]}") — NOT eval or dynamic execution

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
